thought_beat_text: The fingerprint in the clay keeps pulling his eye — someone built this, someone's thumb pressed here, and the stage light hits the foam-core edge at exactly the wrong angle to pretend otherwise. He knows the mission is real, the countdown is real, but the set it's staged on has a seam running straight through the middle of it, and he can't decide if naming that seam would detonate something or finally stop the fuse.
residue_summary: The seam is visible and the clock is running and Snake is caught between the sincerity of the mission and the artifice of the ground it stands on.
image_hint: a thumb-smear in wet clay under a single hard spotlight, edges of the set just visible in peripheral dark
audio_hint: low industrial hum with a single ticking pulse just below the threshold of comfort
